title: Use these Botox injection points for Crows Feet. Botox Anatomy Training
filename: Use these Botox injection points for Crows Feet. Botox Anatomy Training.en.vtt
excerpt: What's the difference between injecting here and here two of these injection points are actually commonly done but one is not for aesthetic medicine the one we usually do with aesthetic medicine is this injection Point here let's have a look here at the anatomy to see why this is…

segments.text: what's the difference between injecting here and here two of these injection points are actually commonly done but one is not for aesthetic medicine the one we usually do with aesthetic medicine is this injection Point here let's have a look here at the anatomy to see why this is where the strongest part of the orbicular oculi muscle resides and when you're treating lateral canl lines it's probably the biggest bang for your buck just to put four units right in that point now a few millimeters either side of it you get really different results and that's where what's worth understanding once you go more lateral which often new injectors do in order to be safer you end up basically missing the muscle and you're injecting Somewhere over the temporalis muscle and this is really going to do almost nothing for your patient perhaps a small amount of atrophy of that muscle if you were to inject deep enough the medial injection is used in oculoplastics and it's used by opthalmologists for people with blos spasm so if the muscle is continuously Contracting you can actually treat that with an injection near this point now

depth is super important here and anyone who's injecting this area will know the anatomy you would hope making sure that you are above the orbicular oculi muscle or maybe just touching it but certainly not on the other side where you're going through the fat into the orbital membrane where because obviously on that side you'll be injecting directly into the orbit and you'll be likely to affect either the lacal gland which resides just here or you may affect the levat pulpy muscle which resides up here or you can see it here and that injection Point therefore is almost never done in aesthetic medicine but you can do it for other reasons so I hope that's helped you understand how small differences in injection points can make big differences to patients if you'd like to learn more about anatomy and boty toxin as well as a whole lot more make sure you sign up to the waiting list for my New Movement it's not open yet but we'll let you know when it is
